news.title: U.S. payrolls rose by 178,000 in March, more than expected; unemployment at 4.3%
news.summary: Nonfarm payrolls were expected to increase by 59,000 in March. The unemployment rate was expected to hold at 4.4%. The economy is expected to grow by 0.3% in the first quarter.

news.title: Trump threatens to destroy Iranian infrastructure, saying its government 'knows what has to be done'
news.summary: The threat comes a day after he said the U.S. military will be hitting Tehran "extremely hard" for the next two or three weeks.

news.title: Trump tariff fallout: Some industries grapple with lingering effects one year later
news.summary: A year after his "liberation day," Trump's trade war has reshaped how companies in industries such as retail and autos are modeling economic and policy risk.

news.title: Meta, Google under attack as court cases bypass 30-year-old legal shield
news.summary: Internet platforms have long been able to rely on special protections to avoid liability for what takes place on their sites. But that may be changing.

news.title: 'Chasing vibes' — OpenAI's M&A strategy gets more confusing with TBPN purchase
news.summary: OpenAI announced it's buying media company TBPN. OpenAI paid $6.4 billion for Jony Ive's nascent devices startup.

news.title: U.S. jobs report shows 178,000 workers were hired in March. But the hiring boomlet is unlikely to last.
news.summary: The U.S. added a greater-than-expected 178,000 jobs in March. The unemployment rate fell a tick to 4.3% — signs that the labor market is holding firm.

news.title: It’s not just patients who are sick of fighting health insurers. Doctors are frustrated, too.
news.summary: Insurance companies get rewarded for their ability to profit from system complexity. The pattern is clear — insurance companies get reward for being able to make money from complexity.

news.title: Stock futures and bitcoin slip, Treasury yields climb, as hot jobs report raises more questions about Fed rate cuts
news.summary: Stock futures have slipped while Treasury yields pressed higher during Friday’s holiday trading session. The hotter-than-expected jobs report raised more questions about whether the Federal Reserve will deliver an interest-rate cut this year.

news.title: Starbucks just made two noteworthy moves to boost its turnaround — but they didn’t help the coffee chain’s stock
news.summary: Starbucks rolls out a bonus and tipping program for its employees. It also hands off a big slice of its store business in China to an investment firm.

news.title: ‘This is an overlooked catastrophe’: Why do so many hospitals not accept Medicare Advantage for cancer patients?
news.summary: Insurers have pushed certain cancer-care centers out of network before the end of the calendar or policy year, the company says. “Insurers are pushing certain cancer care centers.” the company adds.
